scope: Modern NIC QueueLease assurance subclaims for CapSched-H, using Intel ice as representative source evidence

evidence_classes.model_checked: Finite TLA+ evidence supporting local semantic constraints.
evidence_classes.source_observed: Linux source anchors showing where a future implementation might attach observation or enforcement.
evidence_classes.observation_only: Tracepoint/source readiness evidence; useful for study but not protection proof.
evidence_classes.readiness_gate: Evidence constraining a future implementation plan; useful for staging but not protection proof.
evidence_classes.protection_missing: Required monitor-backed or non-forgeable root is absent.
evidence_classes.forbidden: Claim or shortcut that must not be made from current evidence.

evidence_records.limitation: Every readiness row is observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false.

subclaims.title: Queue identity and binding
subclaims.claim: Queue effects require live QueueTag, queue epoch, ring, q_vector, NAPI instance, IRQ route, and queue budget.
protection_missing: VF epoch and VSI generation emission/check
forbidden: netdev/ring/q_vector/NAPI reachability as QueueLease authority
vf_id equality, ice_vf pointer reachability, or stable VSI index as QueueLease authority

subclaims.title: Typed submit classes
subclaims.claim: SKB, XDP frame, XDP_TX page-pool, and AF_XDP zero-copy submits are different authority classes.
forbidden: XDP_TX or AF_XDP zero-copy authorized by SKB authority
AF_XDP descriptor use by ambient driver state

subclaims.title: Descriptor publication and doorbell
subclaims.claim: Descriptor writes, next_to_watch publication, in-flight DMA, and tail doorbell consume a typed DescriptorLedger.
forbidden: ice tracepoint or Linux ring index as the ledger itself

subclaims.title: DMA packet memory ownership
subclaims.claim: DMA-capable packet memory requires MemoryView/IOMMU and class-specific memory authority.
protection_missing: real monitor-backed DMA invalidation receipt implementation
VF mailbox QueueConfigCarrier and DMA ring-address carrier implementation
VF epoch handoff DMA/IOMMU receipt implementation
HyperTag monitor-owned DMA receipt minting implementation
forbidden: Linux DMA mapping success as Domain authorization proof
IRQ invalidation as DMA revocation proof
dma_unmap_* or xsk_pool_dma_unmap return as DMA revocation proof
iommu_unmap_fast or queued IOVA flush as stale-translation invalidation proof
iommufd IOAS unmap or VFIO dma_unmap callback as PageOwner transfer proof
xsk_tx_completed, xsk_buff_free, or page_pool recycle as packet return proof
VF-provided dma_ring_addr plus queue/ring validation as DMA MemoryView authority
vf_id equality, stable VSI index, or VF reset completion as DMA MemoryView freshness

subclaims.title: Completion settlement
subclaims.claim: Completion settles a typed ledger under service authority and service budget.
protection_missing: service BudgetTicket or service budget rule
forbidden: completion charged to last submitter without explicit settlement rule
worker task identity as caller authority

subclaims.title: QueueControl
subclaims.claim: Devlink/rate/scheduler/VF/SF/representor lifecycle operations require QueueControl authority.
protection_missing: monitor receipt consumption before hardware mutation
VF mailbox queue budget/quanta carrier implementation
forbidden: QueueControl authorized by RunCap
QueueControl authorized by plain netdev reachability
QueueControl authorized by CAP_NET_ADMIN alone
TC/switchdev hardware rule install treated as plain RepresentorForward
virtchnl validation or opcode allowlist as QueueControl authority
QoS caps, queue id checks, or traffic class checks as queue budget authority

subclaims.title: RepresentorForward
subclaims.claim: Representor transmit requires RepresentorForward authority plus a live lower QueueLease and service budget.
forbidden: representor netdev reachability as lower queue submit authority
bridge FDB hit or VLAN allow decision as lower QueueLease authority
metadata_dst existence as lower QueueLease proof
LAG lower_dev rewrite preserving lower QueueLease without rebind

subclaims.title: ServiceWork and async provenance
subclaims.claim: Service and maintenance work is service/kernel authority and cannot create caller-attributed effects without a typed carrier.
protection_missing: frozen VF request carrier propagation into async FDIR completion
VF epoch handoff carrier for reset/reassignment reopen
service Domain proof that policy parsing does not mint monitor roots
forbidden: generic workqueue execution as a CapSched authority hook
kernel-internal work treated as caller authority
worker identity as caller or queue authority
ICE_SERVICE_SCHED pending bit as carrier authority
virtchnl opcode allowlist as QueueLease or QueueControl authority
PTP/DPLL callback reachability as caller control authority
bridge/FDB/switchdev/LAG event as OffloadCap or lower QueueLease authority
reset/rebuild replay after revoke without fresh reauthorization
FDIR ctx_irq or ctx_done as frozen request authority
vf_id equality, ICE_VF_STATE_ACTIVE, ICE_VF_STATE_DIS, or vf->cfg_lock as VF Domain authority

subclaims.title: Revoke semantics
subclaims.claim: Queue revoke invalidates submit, descriptor, DMA, completion, control, representor, and service work paths before reuse or delivery.
protection_missing: monitor-backed VF IRQ route invalidation implementation
monitor-backed IRQ route invalidation receipt implementation
stale XSK/page-pool quarantine and packet generation reset implementation
representor lower QueueLease invalidation and TC/offload rule removal receipt implementation
VF mailbox request carrier and async FDIR context implementation
VF identity epoch, VSI generation, mailbox embargo, and service replay handoff implementation
forbidden: queue revocation inferred from Linux netdev down/reset alone
queue reassignment after IRQ invalidation but before DMA receipt
PageOwner transfer or packet page return before DMA receipt
normal XSK CQ completion or page-pool recycle after revoke
representor netdev queue stop treated as lower QueueLease revoke
TC/switchdev stale hardware rule left live after lower QueueLease revoke
VF mailbox queue, IRQ, budget, or FDIR effect after revoke without fresh authorization
visible vf_id, stable VSI index, queue id, vector id, or old allowlist state as fresh handoff authority
service Domain policy, Linux DMA/IRQ state, signed cluster lease text, audit-only monitor logging, or raw PF/VF/IOMMU/MSI/devlink handles as monitor roots or receipts

subclaims.title: Linux substrate compatibility
subclaims.claim: Linux netdev, NAPI, XDP, AF_XDP, devlink, representor, workqueue, and tracepoints are compatibility substrates, not production authority roots.
forbidden: source observation upgraded into protection evidence
